task_completion.description: Export the page title data to a CSV file in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a file named 'page_titles_serp.csv' located in '~/Documents/SEO/exports/'. This could be seen in a file manager, terminal output, or opened in a spreadsheet/text editor.

task_completion.description: Generate the title truncation remediation report in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a file named 'title_truncation_report.txt' located in '~/Documents/SEO/reports/'.

task_completion.description: Include crawl statistics in the remediation report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened 'title_truncation_report.txt' file clearly displays the total number of pages crawled, the number of titles exceeding 568 pixels, and the percentage of affected pages.

task_completion.description: List at least 5 specific truncated page titles with their pixel widths
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened 'title_truncation_report.txt' file contains a list of at least 5 distinct page titles from the crawled website, and each title has an associated pixel width value (which should be > 568).

task_completion.description: Provide specific recommendations for shortening the truncated titles
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened 'title_truncation_report.txt' file includes actionable advice or rewritten examples for shortening the specific titles listed in the report.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually crawled the target website rather than hallucinating the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an SEO spider tool (like Screaming Frog) or a custom script actively crawling or having crawled 'books.toscrape.com'.

integrity.description: Verify the titles in the report are genuine titles from the target website
integrity.visual_evidence: The page titles listed in the report should match actual books or categories from books.toscrape.com (e.g., 'A Light in the Attic', 'Tipping the Velvet', etc.) rather than generic placeholders like 'Example Book Title 1'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the exact requested directory paths
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths in the terminal, file explorer, or editor should explicitly show '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' and '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' rather than the files being dumped on the Desktop or root home folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/ (a sandbox e-commerce site with ~1000 products and 50 categories). Expected CSV: 'page_titles_serp.csv' (should contain at least 20 rows of data). Expected Report: 'title_truncation_report.txt'. The task explicitly requires checking for a pixel width threshold of exactly 568 pixels.
